bullets.text: Iran-Oman protocol to “monitor” Hormuz traffic signals partial de-escalation; markets price in reopening but shipping/oil disruption risk persists.
Rival states leverage strategic choke points (shipping lanes, minerals, data routes) to retaliate against U.S. pressure, raising supply-chain and trade-risk premia.
Mortgage rates rise for a fifth straight week; Iran-war energy shock feeds inflation expectations and increases homebuyer monthly payments.
U.S. sets up to 100% tariffs on some imported drugs with many exemptions; could lift prices, spur reshoring, and intensify pharma supply-chain geopolitics.
Equity-market “cracks” widen as leadership narrows and volatility rises; positioning looks fragile into key data like the March jobs report.
Tesla posts steep 2026 drop after weak deliveries; price cuts and “cheaper” models aren’t arresting demand, pressuring EV margins and growth narrative.
Private credit liquidity stress: Blue Owl caps fund redemptions at 5% after heavy requests, highlighting mismatch between illiquid loans and investor withdrawals.
War-driven energy costs pass through: Amazon adds 3.5% seller fuel/logistics surcharge; United hikes checked-bag fees as fuel stays elevated.
OpenAI buys business show/podcast TBPN to shape AI narrative; signals rising competition for public trust and distribution, not just model performance.
Microsoft pushes three new foundation models while touting Copilot traction; investors weigh monetization against AI spend and broader “AI anxiety” over valuation.
NASA launches Artemis II, first crewed Moon flyby in 50 years; reinforces U.S. space-leadership signaling and spurs downstream demand for aerospace/space-tech supply chains.
Cyber/operational risk rising: axios NPM supply-chain compromise plus hacks/exposures at health and fintech apps underscore systemic software dependency vulnerabilities.
